name: Web Editing
description: Standard for the rich-text / structured editing domain — a browser-aligned way to drive an editable surface that keeps the editing engine (how text is captured and mutated) swappable behind one contract. It defines the Editor Engine Protocol — an engine-swap registry with a native-first default (contenteditable + InputEvent + the editing-surface capabilities, with an editcontext upgrade) over a structured-node-tree internal pivot, so thin adapters (ProseMirror, Lexical, Slate, Quill) plug in behind it. Graduated from #590 (Fork 2 ratified) under epic #618.
tierEvidence: The rich-text/structured-editing domain the named frameworks ProseMirror / Slate / Lexical own; FUI ships the engine adapter (packages/rich-text-editor-slate) behind the Editor Engine Protocol.
